now.state: A bag with $55/dozen ProV1s they can't fully compress
now.quote: I know alot of golfers who won't play with anything but pro v and their swing speed is about 80mph. What a waste.

now.state: Drives landing 210-240 yards and shrinking every year
A closet of half-tried ball brands from seasonal experiments
now.quote: I've been experimenting this year, using Bridgestone e12 & Callaway super soft.

now.state: Two types of balls — the 'real' ball and the 'water' ball
now.quote: Par 5, no water... PROV1. Par 3 over water... Kirkland signature.

after.state: One ball — engineered for their actual swing speed, proven by robot data
5-15 yards recovered that they thought aging took permanently
after.quote: Two years ago, 205 yds. Now averaging 240.

after.state: $30/dozen performance that matches $55 premium at their speed
One ball for every hole — no more risk calculation per shot
after.quote: I buy 10 dozen logo balls in March every year and play them all season.

now.state: Watching drives fall shorter than last season — again
now.quote: I have lost close to 50 yards since then. It is a huge shock.

now.state: Standing in the ball aisle paralyzed by 30 options
now.quote: Currently trying to decide between prov1 (used), Maxfli Tour, or Vice Pro.

now.state: That sinking feeling of a ballooning drive in the wind
now.quote: Every other drive would just whiffleball a mile high.

now.state: Playing partners casually outdrive them with less effort
now.quote: I watch some people make a seemingly effortless swing and develop 110 mph.

after.state: Stepping on the tee knowing the ball matches their swing — data-confirmed
after.quote: I changed based on clubhead speed. Saw immediate results.

after.state: Picking up their iON+ and knowing it's the right ball — no guessing
after.quote: Got my hands on a sleeve and fell in love.

after.state: A drive that flies on the line they aimed — visual alignment confirming the path
after.quote: [Visual alignment is novel — golfers currently draw Sharpie lines]

after.state: Seeing the ball land past where they expected — surprise distance
after.quote: I hit the longest drive I've ever hit on this hole.

now.state: The guy who can't keep up — moving to forward tees, always the shortest drive
now.quote: It's not fair to play with a team as I just can't hold my own.

now.state: The sucker paying $55/dozen because Titleist told him to
now.quote: For 95% of golfers, prov1s are an insane purchase. Pure luxury/flex.

now.state: The guy always trying a new ball — never committed, always searching

after.state: The smart golfer who matched his ball to his swing speed with DATA
after.quote: I have really nice golf equipment without paying those ridiculous prices.

after.state: The one who gained yards while everyone else is losing them
after.quote: Two years ago, 205 yds. Now averaging 240.

after.state: The one his group asks 'what ball are you playing?'

now.state: Frustrated — the distance charts are trending one direction
now.quote: My mind writes checks my body can't cash.

now.state: Exploited — paying more every year for the same ball
now.quote: I'll be damned if I'll ever pay $3-5 for a premium golf ball.

now.state: Uncertain — playing a ball they suspect is wrong but don't know how to verify
now.quote: I definitely am playing a ball that I know I shouldn't for my swing speed.

now.state: Resigned — distance loss feels permanent and accelerating
now.quote: BIG drop between 50 and 60, and again by the time I hit 65.

after.state: Confident — robot data proved this ball works for their speed. Not marketing. Math.
Relieved — off the premium treadmill. Fair price, better performance for them.
after.quote: Nearly the same performance, half the price.

after.state: Hopeful — not ALL the distance was aging. Some came back.
after.quote: I hit the longest drive I've ever hit on this hole.

after.state: Enjoying golf again — when the ball does what you expect, the game is fun
after.quote: I love them. They perform really well.
